title: campanula glomerata f. alba
intro: Campanula glomerata f. alba, also known as White Carpathian Bellflower, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Campanulaceae family, characterized by its white, bell-shaped flowers and heart-shaped leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Campanula glomerata f. alba include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as potentially exhibiting antimicrobial and antifungal properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a decoction or infusion, and can be found in various forms, including tinctures and salves.
